channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Manyara region issues customary land certificates to 150 pastoralist pastoralist women to strengthen land rights
item.description: The Manyara regional government in northern Tanzania has issued customary land certificates to 150 women from pastoral communities as part of efforts to improve land ownership rights and economic empo...

item.title: Cardoso reflects on decisive away goal as Mamelodi Sundowns win CAF Champions League final
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns head coach Miguel Cardoso expressed confidence that his side would eventually find the crucial away goal that helped decide the 2025-26 CAF Champions League final. Speaking after the...

item.title: Air India Express Boeing 737 damaged after runway alignment error during take-off at Muscat airport
item.description: An Air India Express Boeing 737-800 operating flight IX 712 from Muscat to Kannur was involved in a serious runway incident on May 15 during a night departure from Muscat airport. According to sources...

item.title: Profiles of 13 US Service Members Killed During the Iran Conflict
item.description: The article reports on the deaths of 13 US service members linked to Operation Epic Fury, the Pentagon’s designation for the ongoing conflict with Iran. The fatalities occurred across multiple inciden...

item.title: Government to extend financial and relief assistance to families affected by Binondo Parola fire
item.description: The Office of the President in the Philippines has announced that it will provide financial assistance amounting to P30,000 to families affected by the massive fire that recently hit the Parola Compou...

item.title: Francis Tolentino named acting DOLE secretary; profile of former senator and public servant
item.description: Francis Tolentino, a former Philippine senator, has been appointed as the acting secretary of the Department of Labor and Employment (DOLE), according to a report. His latest designation adds another ...

item.title: Richard Avedon’s 1969 Portraits Captured the Lasting Impact of the Shooting of Andy Warhol
item.description: The article examines a series of striking 1969 portraits of artist Andy Warhol taken by renowned photographer Richard Avedon shortly after Warhol survived a near-fatal shooting. The photographs, captu...

item.title: Christine Rose Toquero wins second straight Palarong Pambansa javelin gold for Negros Island Region
item.description: Christine Rose Toquero of the Negros Island Region continued her impressive rise in Philippine athletics after winning the gold medal in the secondary girls’ javelin throw event at the 2026 Palarong P...

item.title: Supreme Court seeks Centre’s response on reforms after NEET paper leak concerns
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has expressed serious concern over the repeated incidents of alleged question paper leaks in the NEET-UG examination process and criticised the National Testing Agency (NTA)...

item.title: Analysis of U.S. Military Operations in Somalia and Global Interventions Across Recent Administrations
item.description: The article presents a critical overview of U.S. military interventions in Somalia and other regions, framing them as part of a long-standing pattern of global military engagement by successive U.S. a...

item.title: Hamilton attributes Canadian GP podium to setup changes and team support at Ferrari
item.description: Lewis Hamilton has expressed renewed optimism after securing second place at the Canadian Grand Prix, marking his strongest result yet while driving for Ferrari. The seven-time Formula One world champ...

item.title: Financial advisors increasingly act as coordinators of clients’ full financial teams
item.description: The article explores how the role of financial advisors in Canada is evolving beyond traditional investment management and planning into what the industry is calling “orchestration.” This new function...

item.title: PNP to evaluate possible revocation of Senator Bato dela Rosa’s firearms license upon formal request
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) has stated that it will properly evaluate and act on any formal request to revoke the firearms license of Senator Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa, once such request is off...

item.title: Attorney-General approvals for war crimes and terrorism cases draw attention over processing times
item.description: Attorney-General Michelle Rowland’s handling of prosecution approvals has come under scrutiny after differences in processing times between high-profile cases were revealed at a Senate committee heari...

item.title: Iran says talks with US have advanced on regional war framework, but key disagreements remain
item.description: Iran on Monday said that important progress has been achieved in ongoing discussions with the United States regarding a possible framework to end the wider regional conflict, but Tehran clarified that...

item.title: Exploring whether banning ticket scalping and resale price caps can reform the ticketing industry
item.description: A growing debate is underway across North America and Europe over whether banning professional ticket scalpers or imposing resale price caps can fix persistent issues in the live events industry. Fans...

item.title: Minority senators seek removal of Imee Marcos ‘con-ass’ remarks from Senate record
item.description: Minority senators in the Philippine Senate have called for the official removal of remarks made by Senator Imee Marcos regarding alleged “con-ass” (constitutional assembly) claims, which they describe...

item.title: CBS halts copyright enforcement on YouTube uploads of Stephen Colbert’s ‘Only in Monroe’ episode amid backlash
item.description: CBS has temporarily suspended copyright takedown notices targeting YouTube uploads of Stephen Colbert’s recent appearance on the public access programme Only in Monroe, following significant public ba...

item.title: Celebrity chef Shankar Santhiram prepares Malaysian Tamil meal for ABH residents in Durban
item.description: Internationally renowned celebrity chef Shankar R. Santhiram, known for his Fire by Shankar restaurant in Malaysia, visited the Aryan Benevolent Homes (ABH) in Chatsworth on Friday, where he prepared ...

item.title: Behind-the-scenes portraits of Steven Spielberg with E.T. during the 1982 production and promotion
item.description: The article presents a collection of behind-the-scenes and promotional portraits of filmmaker Steven Spielberg alongside the E.T. prop during the production and release period of the 1982 film E.T. th...
